channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: UN drug experts say opium production has leveled off in Myanmar, but instability may trigger a rise
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — There has been a slight drop in the production of opium in Myanmar, the world’s biggest source of the illicit drug from which heroin is derived, experts from the United Nations said Thursday, while warning of strong prospects for future expansion of the deadly trade.
item.dc:creator: GRANT PECK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: At least 110 people have died in 7 weeks of post-election protests in Mozambique, rights groups say
item.description: Rights and civil society groups say more than 100 people including children have been killed by security forces in post-election protests in Mozambique that have lasted nearly two months.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hong Kong court convicts 7 men, including ex-lawmaker, of rioting during 2019 protests
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — A Hong Kong judge on Thursday convicted seven people, including a pro-democracy former lawmaker, of rioting during mob violence at a subway station at the height of the city’s anti-government protests in July 2019.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: American who says he crossed into Syria by foot is free after 7 months in detention
item.description: An American who turned up in Syria says he was detained after crossing into the country by foot on a Christian pilgrimage seven months ago.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russian forces edge closer to a key eastern Ukraine city in 'intense' fighting, officials say
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Fighting around the key eastern Ukraine city of Pokrovsk is “extremely intense” after a monthslong Russian push, Ukraine’s top military commander said, with analysts estimating Russian forces are now within just a few kilometers (miles) of the city.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: What to know about martial law and the impeachment vote threatening South Korea's president
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — Is South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol on the verge of being forced out of office for declaring martial law nearly a week ago? The question, which has dogged Yoon through a series of moves by the opposition to end his presidency, will be highlighted Saturday, when parliament seems like
item.dc:creator: FOSTER KLUG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Middle East latest: UN General Assembly demands a ceasefire in Gaza
item.description: The U.N. General Assembly overwhelmingly approved resolutions Wednesday demanding an immediate ceasefire in Gaza and expressing support for the U.N. agency for Palestinian refugees that Israel has moved to ban.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: As tourists discover Finland's Santa Claus Village, some locals call for rules to control the masses
item.description: More than 600,000 people annually visit Santa Claus Village, a winter-themed amusement park in the small town of Rovaniemi in Finland.
item.dc:creator: James Brooks, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: FIFA confirms Saudi Arabia as 2034 World Cup host despite human rights concerns
item.description: ZURICH (AP) — Saudi Arabia was officially confirmed Wednesday by FIFA as host of the 2034 World Cup in men’s soccer, giving the oil-rich kingdom its biggest prize yet for massive spending on global sports driven by Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman.
item.dc:creator: GRAHAM DUNBAR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US troops to remain in Syria, White House says
item.description: The ouster of Bashar al-Assad has left Biden’s outgoing administration to maneuver through a volatile moment in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Israeli strikes on Gaza kill at least 33 including children, Palestinian medics say
item.description: DEIR AL-BALAH, Gaza Strip (AP) — Israeli strikes pounded the Gaza Strip overnight and into Wednesday, with one attack ripping through a home where displaced people were sheltering in the isolated north. The strikes killed at least 33 people including children, Palestinian health officials said.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-defense chief in South Korea tried to kill himself after being arrested over martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s previous defense minister was stopped from attempting suicide while in detention over last week’s martial law declaration, officials said Wednesday, as President Yoon Suk Yeol’s office resisted a police attempt to search the compound.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG and HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 Ukrainian women are pulled alive from rubble hours after Russian missile strike
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Rescue crews pulled two women from rubble several hours after a Russian missile struck a medical clinic in southern Ukraine, killing nine people and wounding 22 others, authorities said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Scholz sets Germany on course for an early election as he requests a confidence vote next week
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Chancellor Olaf Scholz formally set Germany on course for an early election Wednesday by requesting a confidence vote in parliament next week.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Romania's pro-Western parties agree to form a majority government after political turmoil
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Romania’s pro-European parties agreed Wednesday to form a majority government made up of groupings that were traditionally on opposite sides and shutting out far-right nationalists who made significant gains in the election on Dec. 1.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Supporters of Bangladesh Nationalist Party hold protest march as tensions continue with India
item.description: DHAKA, Bangladesh (AP) — Thousands of members of youth and student bodies belonging to the Bangladesh Nationalist Party on Wednesday held a road march procession from Bangladesh’s capital toward its eastern border with India to protest against an attack on a diplomatic mission earlier this month and
item.dc:creator: JULHAS ALAM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mike Huckabee 'commends' Biden support for Israel's self-defense
item.description: Israel said Tuesday it had bombed more than 350 military sites in Syria during the previous 48 hours.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, The Hill

item.title: Gabbard meets with senators amid questions over Russia, Syria
item.description: Former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii), President-elect Trump’s pick for director of national intelligence, met Tuesday with senators on Capitol Hill as she faces concerns from Republicans over her controversial positions sympathetic to U.S.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: China’s sci-fi spherical Death Star-like robot cop uses AI, facial recognition to track criminals
item.description: Chinese robotics company, Logon Technology, unveiled the RT-G autonomous spherical robot which will use AI and facial recognition to aid law enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Netanyahu wants to establish ‘relations’ with Syria but says Israel will attack it if becomes a threat
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu said he wants to establish a relationship with the incoming Syrian government but warned Israel will protect itself amid strikes.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US loans Ukraine $20B from seized Russian assets
item.description: The Treasury Department gave Ukraine a $20 billion loan funded by seized Russian assets Tuesday as part of a broader Group of Seven (G7) effort to boost the country's defense. The money will be transmitted to Ukraine through the World Bank, which received the loan from the U.S. on Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, The Hill

item.title: US will support new Syrian government that renounces terrorism, protects rights: Blinken
item.description: Secretary of State Antony Blinken on Tuesday outlined some of the Biden administration's requirements for supporting a new government in Syria, after a rebel military alliance ousted longtime dictator Bashar al-Assad from power in recent days.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, The Hill

item.title: Collapse of Syria’s Assad regime renews US push to find Austin Tice
item.description: The fall of the Bashar al-Assad and the mass liberation of numerous notorious prisons in Syria has brought renewed hope for the location and return of American Austin Tice, held captive for 12 years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Brazilian President Lula in ICU after surgery to drain head hemorrhage, hospital says
item.description: Brazilian President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva is recovering in an ICU after undergoing surgery to drain an intracranial hemorrhage following a fall at home in October.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Chinese military makes massive deployment around Taiwan to send 'very simple' message
item.description: Taiwan is raising concerns about a massive deployment of Chinese naval ships and military planes in the past 24 hours, saying the build-up could eventually lead to war.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Here's how the US is countering the Islamic State group during Syria's upheaval
item.description: The U.S. has had troops in Syria for the last decade to battle IS. The tumult following a rebel offensive that toppled Assad has raised fears of an Islamic State resurgence.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: US hostage envoy in Beirut to seek information on missing journalist Austin Tice
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. government's top hostage negotiator is in Beirut in hopes of collecting information on the whereabouts of Austin Tice, an American journalist missing in Syria for 12 years, the State Department said Monday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The US believes journalist Austin Tice is alive after disappearing in Syria in 2012, Biden says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden said Sunday that the U.S. government believes missing American journalist Austin Tice, who disappeared 12 years ago near the Syrian capital, is alive and that Washington is committed to bringing him home after Bashar Assad’s ouster from power in Damascus.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Israel deploys paratroopers to Syria in 'defense activities' after fall of Assad
item.description: Israeli forces released footage of their deployment into Syria following the fall of Bashar Assad's regime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Syrian community in Chicago celebrates end of Assad regime
item.description: It was a sea of green flags on Michigan Avenue Sunday as hundreds of Chicagoans celebrated the end of the regime in Syria.
item.dc:creator: Emmy Victor, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea imposes a travel ban on President Yoon over martial law declaration
item.description: The South Korean Justice Ministry imposed an overseas travel ban against President Yoon Suk Yeol on Monday over his martial law declaration last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The rise and fall of Bashar and Asma Assad
item.description: Syrian President Bashar Assad and his wife Asma started as a couple that could have broken the country's tyrant regime, though the opposite happened.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says Russia abandoned Syria's Assad, never should have been involved
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump said on Sunday that Russia's abandonment of Syrian President Bashar al-Assad led to his downfall.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Syrian dictator Bashar Assad flees into exile as Islamist rebels conquer country
item.description: A lightning Syrian Islamist offensive over just days contributed to the demise of the over 50-year reign of the Assad Baathist regime in Damascus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Italian nun among 25 arrested in raid against ‘Ndrangheta mafia
item.description: The nun, 57, is also well-known for her service in Milan's run-down districts. In February she was one of the recipients of the "Golden Panettone", an annual Milanese civic award.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Trump urges US to stay out of Syrian civil war, blaming Obama for failure as Islamists close in on capital
item.description: Amid the rapid offensive of Islamist rebels in their campaign to dislodge Syrian dictator Assad, President-elect Donal Trump has urged non-intervention by the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Amnesty International says Israel committing genocide in Gaza. Israel rejects allegations
item.description: The offensive is among the deadliest and most destructive since World War II, and has destroyed vast areas of the besieged coastal territory.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9
